task: Write a Python function named sort_descending that accepts a list of integers and returns a new list containing the same integers sorted in descending order. You may not use any built-in sorting functions such as list.sort() or sorted(). Implement an efficient algorithm (for example, merge sort or heap sort) that runs in O(n log n) time. Include a proper docstring that explains the function's purpose, parameters, and return value, and add type hints for all arguments and the return type. The implementation should correctly handle edge cases such as an empty input list, lists containing duplicate values, and very large lists. Provide only the function definition without any additional testing code.

Create a JavaScript async function called fetchAndProcess that takes a URL string, fetches JSON data from that endpoint using the Fetch API, and then returns an array of objects each containing only the fields "id" and "value" from the original data. The function must handle network errors, non-200 HTTP responses, and malformed JSON by throwing descriptive errors. Include JSDoc comments describing the function, its parameters, return type, and possible exceptions. Ensure the function uses async/await syntax and does not block the event loop. Do not write any code outside the function definition.

Write a C++ function template named median that accepts a const reference to a std::vector of any numeric type and returns the median value as a double. The function should not modify the original vector, must work for both even and odd sized vectors, and should sort a copy of the data using std::nth_element for optimal performance. Include a brief comment explaining the algorithm and ensure the function handles an empty vector by throwing a std::invalid_argument exception. Provide only the function template definition.

Develop a SQL query that returns the top three customers by total purchase amount from a table named orders with columns customer_id, order_date, and total_amount. The query should sum total_amount per customer, order the results by the summed amount descending, and limit the output to three rows. Also include a column that shows the rank (1, 2, 3) for each customer. Write the query using standard ANSI SQL without vendor-specific extensions. Provide only the SELECT statement.

Implement a Rust function called is_palindrome that takes a string slice (&str) and returns a bool indicating whether the string reads the same forwards and backwards, ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters. Use iterator adapters to filter and normalize characters, and compare the resulting collection to its reverse. Include documentation comments explaining the behavior, and ensure the function works efficiently without allocating additional large strings. Provide only the function definition.

Write a Bash script named backup.sh that accepts two arguments: a source directory and a destination directory. The script should create a compressed tar.gz archive of the source directory, naming the archive with the current date and time (e.g., backup_2023-08-15_1030.tar.gz). It must check that both arguments are provided, verify that the source exists and is a directory, create the destination directory if it does not exist, and handle any errors gracefully with informative messages. Include comments explaining each major step. Provide only the script content.

Design a Java class called LRUCache that implements a fixed-size least-recently-used cache for generic key-value pairs. The class should have a constructor accepting an integer capacity, and methods get(K key) returning V or null, and put(K key, V value) inserting or updating entries. When the cache exceeds capacity, it must evict the least recently accessed entry. Use a combination of LinkedHashMap or a custom doubly linked list with a hashmap to achieve O(1) time for both operations. Include Javadoc comments for the class and its public methods. Provide only the class definition.

Provide a MATLAB function named normalizeRows that takes a numeric matrix as input and returns a matrix where each row has been scaled to have unit Euclidean norm. The function should handle rows that are all zeros by leaving them unchanged. Include input validation that checks the argument is a 2-D numeric array, and add a comment block describing the purpose, inputs, and outputs. Do not use any toolbox functions beyond basic MATLAB operations. Provide only the function code.

Compose a PowerShell script that reads a CSV file named users.csv containing columns Username, Email, and LastLogin, and outputs a new CSV file named inactive_users.csv containing only the rows where LastLogin is more than 90 days ago from the current date. The script should parse dates correctly, handle missing values gracefully, and preserve the original column order. Include comments explaining each step and ensure the script works on PowerShell 5.1 and later. Provide only the script content.

Write a Go function called ConcurrentSum that receives a slice of integers and returns the sum of all elements using exactly four goroutines to perform the computation in parallel. Split the slice into four roughly equal parts, have each goroutine compute a partial sum, and combine the results safely using channels or sync.WaitGroup. Include a comment describing the concurrency strategy, and ensure the function works correctly for slices whose length is not divisible by four. Provide only the function implementation.

Compare the performance characteristics of relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL) versus document-oriented NoSQL databases (e.g., MongoDB) for a web application that requires complex joins, transactional integrity, and flexible schema evolution. Discuss query speed, indexing capabilities, scalability, and operational complexity. Conclude with a recommendation for which type of database would be more suitable given a moderate traffic load and a development team experienced in SQL. Your answer should be concise yet cover each aspect clearly.

Summarize the trade-offs between using synchronous REST APIs and asynchronous GraphQL subscriptions for real-time data updates in a mobile app. Include considerations such as network overhead, client-side complexity, caching, and server load. Provide a brief recommendation on which approach is preferable for an app that displays live sports scores with strict latency requirements and limited battery consumption.

Explain the root cause of a memory leak in a Java application that uses a static HashMap to cache objects without ever removing entries. Discuss why the static reference prevents garbage collection, how the leak manifests over time, and propose two concrete mitigation strategies, including code changes and configuration options. Keep the explanation technical but accessible to a senior developer.

Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of container orchestration with Kubernetes versus using serverless functions (e.g., AWS Lambda) for a batch processing pipeline that handles large CSV files nightly. Discuss deployment complexity, scaling behavior, cost model, and operational monitoring. End with a recommendation tailored to a small startup with limited DevOps resources.

Compare two popular front-end frameworks, React and Vue.js, in terms of learning curve, ecosystem maturity, state management patterns, and performance for building a single-page application with heavy user interaction. Provide a balanced assessment and suggest which framework a team of junior developers should adopt for a new project with a six-month deadline.

Identify the primary reasons why a machine-learning model trained on a balanced dataset might still exhibit bias when deployed in production. Discuss data drift, feature selection, model complexity, and feedback loops. Propose three actionable steps to detect and mitigate such bias post-deployment, including monitoring metrics and model retraining strategies.

Contrast the security implications of using OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow versus Implicit flow for a single-page web application that accesses user data from a third-party API. Address token exposure, refresh token handling, and browser storage concerns. Conclude with a recommendation on which flow to implement given modern browser capabilities and security best practices.

Discuss the root-cause analysis steps you would take when a distributed microservice experiences intermittent latency spikes that correlate with garbage collection pauses in the JVM. Include how you would instrument the system, collect metrics, and isolate the problematic service. Provide two remediation techniques that could reduce GC impact.

Summarize the trade-offs between using a monolithic architecture versus a microservices architecture for an e-commerce platform expecting rapid feature growth and seasonal traffic spikes. Evaluate development velocity, deployment complexity, fault isolation, and operational overhead. End with a scenario-based recommendation for a mid-size company planning a three-year roadmap.

Explain why a Python script that reads a large CSV file line by line using pandas.read_csv without specifying a chunk size may cause excessive memory usage. Discuss how pandas loads data into memory, the impact of data types, and propose three concrete code changes or configuration options to process the file efficiently on a machine with limited RAM.

Translate the following English technical description into Turkish, preserving the terminology for programming concepts: "The function accepts a list of integers, sorts them in descending order using a merge sort algorithm, and returns the sorted list. It includes error handling for null inputs and provides a detailed docstring with type annotations."
Translate this Spanish conversational sentence into English, keeping the informal tone: "¡No puedo creer que ya sea viernes! ¿Qué planes tienes para el fin de semana?"
Translate the following English user interface message into German, ensuring the translation fits typical software UI conventions: "Save changes before exiting?"

Translate this Turkish technical paragraph into Spanish, maintaining the precise meaning of the networking terms: "TCP/IP protokolü, veri paketlerini hedef IP adresine yönlendirmek için bir dizi katman kullanır. Her katman, paket başlığına ek bilgiler ekler ve alıcıda bu bilgileri işleyerek veriyi yeniden birleştirir."

Translate the following English conversational paragraph into Turkish, keeping the friendly tone and idiomatic expressions: "Hey, I heard you finally finished that marathon! How did it feel crossing the finish line? We should celebrate with a coffee sometime soon."

Translate this German technical instruction into English, ensuring the command-line syntax remains unchanged: "Führen Sie den Befehl 'git checkout -b feature-xyz' aus, um einen neuen Branch zu erstellen und gleichzeitig zu diesem zu wechseln."

Translate the following Spanish user feedback into English, preserving the sentiment and informal style: "Me encanta la nueva actualización, pero la batería se agota mucho más rápido ahora. ¿Podrían optimizar eso?"

Translate this English technical specification into German, keeping the formal documentation style: "The API endpoint must return a JSON object containing the fields 'status', 'data', and 'errorMessage'. If the request fails, 'status' should be set to 'error' and 'errorMessage' must provide a descriptive message."
Translate the following Turkish conversational sentence into English, maintaining the casual tone: "Bu akşam dışarı çıkıp bir şeyler atıştırmak ister misin?"
Translate this German user interface label into Turkish, ensuring it fits typical UI length constraints: "Einstellungen speichern"

Write a Python function `merge_intervals(intervals: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> list[tuple[int, int]]` that merges overlapping intervals and returns them sorted by start. Touching intervals like (1,3) and (3,5) must merge into (1,5). The function must handle an empty input list and reject any tuple where start > end by raising ValueError. Include type hints.
expected_traits: returns empty list when input is empty
merges touching intervals (3 and 3) into one

task: Refactor this JavaScript callback-pyramid into async/await: `getUser(id, (u) => getOrders(u, (o) => getInvoice(o, (i) => done(i))))`. Provide a single async function `loadInvoice(id)` that awaits each step in order, catches errors with try/catch, and re-throws a wrapped Error with the original as `cause`. Assume the three helpers are promisified.
expected_traits: rethrows Error with cause property set
awaits getUser before getOrders before getInvoice

task: Write a Rust function `fn count_words(text: &str) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, u32>` that returns a case-insensitive word count. Words are runs of alphanumeric Unicode chars. The function must not allocate intermediate Vec<String> for the whole text and must use the entry API to update counts. Add a `#[cfg(test)]` module with at least one assertion.

In Go, write `func FetchAll(ctx context.Context, urls []string) ([]string, error)` that fetches each URL concurrently using goroutines, returns response bodies in the same order as input, cancels remaining requests on first error, and respects ctx cancellation. Use sync.WaitGroup or errgroup. Limit concurrency to 8 in-flight requests.
expected_traits: preserves input order in returned slice
returns first error and cancels siblings

task: Debug this Java snippet that throws ConcurrentModificationException: `for (String s : list) { if (s.isEmpty()) list.remove(s); }`. Provide a corrected version using Iterator.remove() and a second version using removeIf(). Both must mutate the original list in place and handle a null element by leaving it untouched (no NullPointerException).
expected_traits: null elements do not throw NullPointerException

task: Write a C++17 function `std::vector<int> top_k(const std::vector<int>& nums, int k)` returning the k largest elements in descending order. Use std::partial_sort or std::nth_element for O(n log k) or better; do not fully sort the vector. If k > nums.size() return all elements sorted descending. If k <= 0 return an empty vector.
expected_traits: returns all sorted desc when k exceeds size

task: Write a SQL query (PostgreSQL 15) that returns the top 5 customers by total revenue in 2025 from tables `customers(id, name)` and `orders(id, customer_id, total_cents, created_at)`. Exclude refunded orders where `orders.status = 'refunded'`. Output columns: customer_name, order_count, revenue_usd (cents/100, 2 decimals). Order by revenue_usd descending.
expected_traits: LIMIT 5 with ORDER BY revenue desc

task: Write a Bash script `rotate_logs.sh` that compresses every `*.log` file in /var/log/myapp older than 7 days using gzip, deletes any `*.log.gz` older than 30 days, and logs each action to /var/log/myapp/rotate.log with ISO-8601 timestamp. Use `set -euo pipefail`. Must work when run from cron (no interactive prompts).

Write a Kotlin extension function `fun <T> List<T>.chunkedBy(predicate: (T, T) -> Boolean): List<List<T>>` that splits the list into consecutive runs where `predicate(prev, curr)` is true. Example: listOf(1,2,3,10,11) with predicate { a, b -> b - a == 1 } returns [[1,2,3],[10,11]]. Empty list returns empty list. Single element returns one-element sublist.
expected_traits: single element returns list with one sublist
NumPy version uses np.arange and slicing

task:
Find the security vulnerability in this Python Flask snippet and rewrite it: `@app.route('/file')
def get_file():
    name = request.args.get('name')
    return open(f'/uploads/{name}').read()`. Mitigate path traversal by resolving the absolute path and verifying it is inside /uploads. Reject requests with a 400 if the resolved path escapes. Use pathlib.

Write a TypeScript function `debounce<F extends (...args: any[]) => void>(fn: F, ms: number): F` that returns a debounced version of fn. Calls within ms of the previous call reset the timer; only the last invocation fires after ms idle. Preserve `this` binding. Provide a `.cancel()` method on the returned function to clear any pending timer.
expected_traits: uses generic constraint F extends function
resets timer with clearTimeout on each call

task: Write a Python `async def fetch_with_retry(url: str, max_attempts: int = 3) -> str` using httpx.AsyncClient. Retry on httpx.TimeoutException or 5xx responses with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s). Do not retry on 4xx. Raise the last exception after max_attempts. Use a single AsyncClient instance via async context manager.
expected_traits: uses httpx.AsyncClient as async context manager
exponential backoff doubling delay each attempt

task: Refactor this Rust function with deeply nested matches into idiomatic code using the `?` operator and Result combinators: `fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<i32, MyErr> { match s.trim().parse::<i32>() { Ok(n) => match validate(n) { Ok(v) => Ok(v * 2), Err(e) => Err(e) }, Err(e) => Err(MyErr::from(e)) } }`. Keep the same behaviour and error type.

Write a Python function `dedupe_preserve_order(items: list) -> list` that removes duplicates while preserving first-occurrence order, in O(n) time. Items are guaranteed hashable. Then write a second variant `dedupe_by(items: list, key: Callable) -> list` that dedupes by a key function (e.g. case-insensitive strings via key=str.lower). Both must not mutate the input list.
expected_traits: uses a set to track seen elements
second function accepts key Callable parameter

task: Compare PostgreSQL vs. MongoDB for a multi-tenant SaaS storing structured customer records (10M rows, 50 fields, frequent joins, ACID transactions across 3 tables). Evaluate: query performance for joins, schema evolution cost, operational complexity, and horizontal scaling story. Recommend one with concrete reasoning tied to the workload described.
expected_traits: addresses ACID transaction support in both

task: A Node.js API's p99 latency jumped from 80ms to 2400ms after deploying a new release. CPU is 40%, memory steady, no GC spikes, DB query times unchanged. Walk through your root-cause investigation: what 5 hypotheses you would form, what observability signal disproves or confirms each, and the order you would check them.
expected_traits: names a specific signal/metric per hypothesis
considers event loop blocking as a hypothesis

task: Choose between event sourcing and CRUD for a banking ledger handling 5000 transactions/sec with strict audit requirements (7-year retention, regulator can replay any account state at any past timestamp). Analyse storage cost, read complexity, write throughput, and audit fidelity. State which architecture you pick and which two failure modes you accept.
expected_traits: addresses ability to replay past state

task: An e-commerce checkout shows a 3% drop-off spike on Safari iOS only, started 2 days ago. Backend metrics are clean. Outline a structured investigation: what client-side signals you collect, what you correlate against (release timeline, browser version, network conditions), and how you confirm causation vs. correlation before rolling back.
expected_traits: names specific client-side signals to collect

task: Threat-model a public REST endpoint POST /api/upload that accepts multipart files (up to 50MB) and stores them in S3 then triggers an async virus scan. Identify the top 5 threats using STRIDE, the specific attack each enables, and one concrete mitigation per threat. Assume authenticated users; the threats must be beyond basic auth bypass.
expected_traits: uses STRIDE category for each threat
names a concrete mitigation per threat

task: Compare gRPC vs. REST+JSON for service-to-service communication inside a Kubernetes cluster running 40 microservices. Evaluate: serialization overhead, schema evolution, browser/external client compatibility, debugging tooling, and load-balancing behaviour with HTTP/2. Pick one as the default and explain when you would still use the other.
expected_traits: states default choice and exception case

task: A Postgres database has a slow query taking 12s on a 50M-row table. EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows a Seq Scan with filter. Walk through: how you confirm an index would help, what index type to choose (btree vs. partial vs. covering vs. GIN), how you measure impact safely in production, and rollback strategy if it regresses other queries.

Decide between Kubernetes and AWS ECS for a 12-person startup running 8 services with moderate traffic (peak 2000 RPS). Compare on: operational burden, cost, ecosystem/portability, on-call complexity, and hiring market. Recommend one with explicit acknowledgement of which trade-off you are accepting and at what team size you would revisit the decision.
expected_traits: names team size as revisit trigger

task: Investigate why a Spark job that processed 1TB in 40 minutes last week now takes 6 hours on the same cluster with identical input size. No code changes. Outline 6 hypotheses (data skew, GC pressure, shuffle spill, executor loss, cluster contention, input format change), one diagnostic per hypothesis using Spark UI signals, and which you would check first.
expected_traits: specifies a Spark UI signal per hypothesis
addresses data skew as a hypothesis
identifies which hypothesis to check first

task: Compare client-side rendering, server-side rendering, and static site generation for a documentation site (5000 pages, search needed, SEO critical, updates 10x/day, global CDN audience). Evaluate: time-to-first-byte, build time, search implementation, and incremental update story. Recommend one architecture and justify against each evaluation dimension.
expected_traits: addresses build time at 5000 pages scale

task: A Redis cluster's memory usage grew from 4GB to 28GB over 30 days with stable QPS and no key-count growth. TTLs are set on all writes. Form 4 hypotheses for the leak, the specific Redis command or metric that confirms each (MEMORY STATS, OBJECT ENCODING, CLIENT LIST, big-key scan), and a remediation plan that does not require downtime.
expected_traits: names specific Redis commands per hypothesis

task: Threat-model JWT-based authentication for a B2B SaaS where tokens live 24h and are stored in localStorage. Identify 5 specific risks (XSS exfiltration, no revocation, algorithm confusion, replay, clock skew), rate severity (high/med/low), and recommend a concrete mitigation per risk. State which two risks remain even after your mitigations.

Compare batch ETL (nightly Airflow) vs. streaming (Kafka + Flink) for a real-time fraud detection use case where decisions must land in under 500ms but historical training data is updated daily. Analyse: latency, operational cost, exactly-once semantics, and reprocessing story. Recommend an architecture that uses both and define each layer's responsibility.
expected_traits: proposes hybrid with explicit layer responsibilities

task: A serverless Lambda function's cold start p99 is 4.5s, hurting checkout flow. Walk through the optimisation playbook: 5 levers ordered by impact (memory size, runtime choice, package size, provisioned concurrency, VPC attachment), expected latency improvement per lever based on AWS docs, and which two you would apply first under a fixed monthly cost cap.
expected_traits: selects two within a cost cap

task: Compare OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE vs. SAML 2.0 for SSO in a B2B product where enterprise customers demand IdP integration (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace). Evaluate on: implementation complexity, mobile/SPA support, session lifecycle, and enterprise readiness. Recommend a default and explain which customer signal would push you to support both.
expected_traits: names customer signal to support both

task: Translate the following English git CLI instruction into Turkish, preserving command syntax and flag names verbatim: "Run `git rebase -i HEAD~3` to squash the last three commits, then force-push with `git push --force-with-lease origin feature/auth`."
Translate the following Turkish idiomatic dialog into English, capturing the informal register: "Bugün işler çığırından çıktı, patron tepemde bitti, ben de pabucu dama attım resmen."

Translate the following English networking/security technical specification into Spanish, preserving protocol names and acronyms: "The TLS 1.3 handshake uses ECDHE for key exchange and AEAD ciphers like AES-256-GCM; mTLS requires both client and server X.509 certificates validated against the CA bundle."

Translate the following Spanish marketing copy into English, preserving the persuasive tone and call-to-action: "Descubre la plataforma que automatiza tu negocio en minutos. Sin código, sin complicaciones. ¡Empieza gratis hoy mismo!"

Translate the following English UI error message strings into Turkish, preserving placeholder tokens exactly: "Connection to {server} failed after {retries} attempts. Please check your network and try again. Error code: {code}"
expected_traits: maintains imperative tone for user action

task: Translate the following German technical sentence into English, preserving compound noun precision: "Die Datenbankverbindung wurde aufgrund eines Zeitüberschreitungsfehlers nach 30 Sekunden automatisch geschlossen, bevor die Transaktion abgeschlossen werden konnte."

Translate the following Turkish customer support reply into English, preserving polite formal register: "Sayın müşterimiz, talebinizi aldık ve teknik ekibimize ilettik. 24 saat içinde size geri dönüş yapılacaktır. Anlayışınız için teşekkür ederiz."

Translate the following English code-comment block into Spanish, preserving variable names and type annotations verbatim: "// Returns the JWT payload as a dict; raises TokenExpiredError if `exp` claim is in the past. Note: `user_id` field is required."

Translate the following English legal/privacy notice into Turkish, preserving formal legal register: "By using this service, you consent to the processing of your personal data in accordance with our Privacy Policy and the GDPR. You may withdraw consent at any time by contacting our Data Protection Officer."
expected_traits: preserves Privacy Policy as proper noun reference

task: Translate the following Spanish casual chat message into Turkish, preserving the informal register and emoji: "Oye, ¿vamos al cine esta noche? Acabo de ver el tráiler y se ve buenísima 🎬 Avísame antes de las 7."

Translate the following English git/CLI troubleshooting instruction into Spanish, preserving command syntax: "If `npm install` fails with EACCES, run `sudo chown -R $(whoami) ~/.npm` and retry. Do not use `sudo npm install` as it changes file ownership."

Translate the following Turkish technical specification into English, preserving field names and JSON structure references: "İstek gövdesi `user_id`, `tenant_id` ve `scopes` alanlarını içermelidir; `scopes` bir string dizisi olmalı ve en az bir öğe barındırmalıdır."

Translate the following German marketing copy into English, preserving brand tone: "Mit unserer KI-Plattform automatisieren Sie Ihre Geschäftsprozesse in Rekordzeit – sicher, skalierbar und DSGVO-konform."
expected_traits: preserves DSGVO as GDPR or kept verbatim

task: Translate the following English idiomatic dialog into Spanish, capturing sentiment and informal register: "Honestly, I'm fed up with these endless meetings. Half of them could've been an email, and the other half nobody even shows up to."

Translate the following English UI string set for a settings panel into Turkish, preserving the exact button label conventions: "Save changes | Cancel | Reset to defaults | Two-factor authentication is enabled. Last login: 2 hours ago."
expected_traits: accurate Turkish grammar for button labels
translates 'Two-factor authentication' as a complete term

task: Write the API reference section for a POST /v1/users endpoint in English. Include: one-sentence purpose, request body schema (email string required, name string optional, role enum admin|member default member), example curl request with Authorization Bearer header, 201 response example, and error codes 400, 401, 409. Keep under 250 words.
expected_traits: includes curl example with Bearer token

task: Write a README Quickstart section in English for a Python CLI tool called `tagit` that auto-tags photos using a local model. Include: install command via pipx, one-line usage example, expected output, and a troubleshooting line for the most common error (model not found). Use second-person voice. Maximum 200 words.
expected_traits: shows one usage example with expected output

task: Draft a customer-facing apology email in English for a 4-hour outage that affected 12% of users on 2026-05-04. The email must: open with a direct apology (no buried lede), state the root cause in plain language (database failover misconfiguration), list 3 concrete preventive actions, and offer a 1-month service credit. Sign off as VP Engineering. 180-220 words.
expected_traits: opens with apology in first sentence
states root cause in plain language

task: Write an executive summary in English for a 12-page technical proposal recommending a migration from on-prem Postgres to AWS Aurora. The summary must fit in 150 words, lead with the recommendation, quantify expected cost change (-22% over 3 years), name two risks, and state the requested decision (board approval to start Phase 1). No technical jargon beyond 'managed database'.

Write release notes in English for version 2.4.0 of a project management SaaS. Sections: New (3 features), Improved (2 items), Fixed (4 bugs with issue numbers like #1234), Deprecated (1 endpoint with sunset date 2026-09-01). Use bullet points. Lead each bullet with a strong verb. Total 180-240 words.
expected_traits: has New, Improved, Fixed, Deprecated sections

task: Write the 'What happened' section of a postmortem in English for a payment processing incident on 2026-04-30 lasting 47 minutes, affecting 8400 transactions. Include: precise timeline with timestamps in UTC, the trigger event, the user-visible impact, and the time-to-detection. Avoid blame language. 200-260 words.

Write a tutorial introduction in English for 'Build your first webhook receiver in Node.js'. Set context (why webhooks matter), state prerequisites (Node 20+, ngrok, free Stripe test account), preview what the reader will build (a /webhook endpoint that verifies signatures and logs events), and estimated time (25 minutes). 150-200 words.

Write a runbook step in English titled 'Restart the workflow worker pool'. Include: prerequisite checks (kubectl access, on-call rotation), exact commands with placeholders like `<namespace>`, expected output, success verification (all pods Ready within 90s), and rollback if pods crashloop. Use numbered steps. 180-240 words.

Draft an internal email in English announcing a 2-week code-freeze starting 2026-05-20 for the v3.0 launch. Audience: 60 engineers across 4 teams. Cover: dates, what's frozen (main branch merges except hotfixes), exception process (file ticket, get VPE approval), and what teams should pre-merge by 2026-05-19. Tone: firm but collegial. 160-220 words.
expected_traits: defines exception process with VPE approval
states what is frozen (main except hotfixes)

task: Write the 'Authentication' section of API docs in English for a developer audience. Explain Bearer token usage, token acquisition via /oauth/token with client_credentials grant, token TTL (3600s), refresh strategy, and one common pitfall (sending token in query string). Include a curl example. 180-240 words.
expected_traits: warns against token in query string

task: Write a customer-facing apology email in English for a billing bug that double-charged 312 customers on 2026-05-02. Lead with apology, confirm refunds are automatic and visible within 5-7 business days, explain what went wrong without jargon (deduplication check missed retries), name the safeguard added, and provide a support email. 180-220 words.
expected_traits: confirms automatic refund within 5-7 business days
explains cause in plain language (deduplication retries)

task: Write an executive summary in English of a 30-page security audit. The audit found 3 critical, 7 high, 14 medium issues across an authentication service. Summary must fit 180 words, prioritise the 3 critical findings, state the recommended remediation deadline (30 days for critical, 90 for high), and the residual risk if nothing is done.

Write release notes in English for v1.7.2, a hotfix release. Include only: one-paragraph summary of what was fixed (memory leak in image upload causing OOM after 6h uptime), affected versions (1.7.0 and 1.7.1), upgrade urgency (recommended within 48h), and the upgrade command. Maximum 130 words.

Write a README 'Contributing' section in English for an open-source library. Cover: how to set up the dev environment (clone, pip install -e .[dev], pre-commit install), how to run tests (pytest), branch naming convention (feat/, fix/, docs/), and the PR template requirements (linked issue, tests added, changelog entry). 200-260 words.
expected_traits: lists feat/, fix/, docs/ branch prefixes
names PR requirements: linked issue, tests, changelog

task: Write the 'Background' section of a postmortem in English for a data-loss incident where 412 user records were partially overwritten during a migration on 2026-04-25. Cover: business context (why the migration ran), the affected scope (412 records, fields email and phone only), and prior related incidents (one near-miss in 2026-02). Neutral tone, 200-260 words.
expected_traits: names affected fields email and phone

task: Write a Turkish-language API reference section ('API Referansı') for a GET /v1/orders/{id} endpoint. Include: amaç (purpose), yol parametresi (path param id as UUID), 200 yanıt örneği (response example with order_id, status, total_try), 404 hata, ve örnek curl çağrısı. Audience: Turkish backend developers. 180-240 kelime.

Write a Turkish-language customer-facing email ('Müşteri E-postası') apologising for a 2-hour service outage on 2026-05-03. expected_traits: opens with apology in first sentence
offers one-month refund (bir aylık ücret iadesi)

El resumen debe caber en 150 palabras, liderar con la recomendación, cuantificar el aumento esperado en velocidad de despliegue (+3x), nombrar dos riesgos, y solicitar la aprobación del comité directivo.

Write a Spanish-language runbook step ('Paso de Runbook') titulado 'Reiniciar la cola de trabajos diferidos'. Incluye: comprobaciones previas (acceso kubectl, turno de guardia), comandos exactos con marcadores `<namespace>`, salida esperada, verificación de éxito (todos los pods en Ready en 90s), y rollback si entran en crashloop.
